title: St. Francis of Assisi
content:
Would I might wake St. Francis in you all,
        Brother of birds and trees, God's Troubadour,
        Blinded with weeping for the sad and poor;
        Our wealth undone, all strict Franciscan men,
        Come, let us chant the canticle again
        Of mother earth and the enduring sun.
        God make each soul the lonely leper's slave;
        God make us saints, and brave.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Vachel Lindsay delivers a powerful performance in "St. Francis of Assisi"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
